title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Repository Trace: Gita Format Patch Am Bundle
summary: An urgent repair interrupts mixed staged, unstaged, untracked, and ignored experiments; some work must remain visible, some move across clones, and no secret may enter an object. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita format patch am bundle increment in work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare stash, diff, format-patch/am, and bundle transfer by metadata, prerequisites, reviewability, trust, and connectivity.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Format Patch Am Bundle
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G66 is a theory in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G66 is a worked example in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. A defensible solution applies Gita Format Patch Am Bundle, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects

steps.title: Build Gita Format Patch Am Bundle evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-format-patch-am-bundle-1ihhm6" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Format Patch Am Bundle into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-format-patch-am-bundle-1ihhm6
# scenario-key: trace-1ihhm6 probe-6mhhi1 boundary-hhm61i owner-1ihhm6x fault-1ihhm6z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=b00b1m6
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=c0ffee6
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects
